title: OpenClinXR IWSDK Spike Plan Package
citation: OpenClinXR package @openclinxr/iwsdk-spike, created 2026-05-04 to encode Meta Immersive Web SDK adoption gates and agent verification runbooks without adding IWSDK runtime dependencies.
permitted_uses: Internal implementation planning for an isolated Meta Immersive Web SDK spike, including package posture, source traceability, local agent verification, and production-adoption blockers.
claims_supported: The OpenClinXR workspace now has a project-owned @openclinxr/iwsdk-spike package that contains typed spike planning data but does not depend on @iwsdk packages.
The package restricts IWSDK experimentation to apps/ui-xr-iwsdk-spike/ and packages/openclinxr/iwsdk-spike/ while keeping production XR roots blocked.

The package records source-backed candidate packages, including @iwsdk/core, @iwsdk/xr-input, @iwsdk/glxf, @iwsdk/locomotor, @iwsdk/vite-plugin-dev, @iwsdk/vite-plugin-gltf-optimizer, @iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml, and @iwsdk/vite-plugin-metaspatial.
The package records blocked or unattended-only posture for @iwsdk/create, @iwsdk/reference, @iwsdk/starter-assets, and @meta-quest/hzdb.
The package records a Codex-oriented verification runbook that checks iwsdk dev status, then xr_get_session_status, then XR entry, screenshot, scene hierarchy, controller select, and console logs.

The package records a managed-browser evidence contract and evaluator that distinguish agent-mode managed Playwright evidence from normal-browser session independence, and distinguish oversight/collaborate visible Playwright behavior from automatic normal-browser behavior.

The package records an aggregate IWSDK agent-tooling evidence evaluator that requires adapter sync, 32-tool MCP inventory, required MCP category coverage, the minimal smoke subset, managed-browser evidence, and absence of blocked optional MCP server actions.
The root workspace exposes pnpm iwsdk:browser:evidence as a JSON scoring command for future IWSDK managed-browser evidence files.
The root workspace exposes pnpm iwsdk:agent-tooling:evidence as a JSON scoring command for future aggregate IWSDK MCP evidence files.
The root workspace exposes pnpm iwsdk:sidecar:metrics as a JSON scoring command for future install footprint, bundle, console, and Quest performance metrics files.

The root workspace exposes pnpm iwsdk:workspace:posture as a JSON workspace scan for IWSDK dependency/import isolation, blocked script actions, blocked lockfile packages, sharp/libvips-style blocked transitive lockfile packages, sidecar lockfile importer parity, sidecar approval state, and package-manager controls before any committed IWSDK sidecar can be treated as approved.
The root workspace exposes pnpm iwsdk:evidence as a no-install JSON report for current IWSDK contract-only evidence debt.
The root workspace exposes pnpm iwsdk:evidence:validate as a no-install structure check for the latest committed IWSDK evidence-contract JSON snapshot.

docs/openclinxr/iwsdk-evidence-contract-2026-05-04.json records the current IWSDK contract-only evidence report and preinstall package policy without installing IWSDK packages, changing MCP config, or requiring headset state.

The package records machine-readable sidecar and production readiness thresholds for installed footprint, injected IWSDK dev runtime size, JS bundle size, bundle delta versus apps/ui-xr, console errors, foreground Quest preflight readiness, Quest 3 FPS, p95 frame time, and controller-select latency.
The root workspace exposes pnpm iwsdk:verify as an explicit opt-in policy lane for IWSDK spike planning checks without adding IWSDK installation assumptions to the default pnpm verify gate.
The package exposes a Codex MCP adapter template and docs/openclinxr/iwsdk-codex-mcp-runbook.md records the local-only setup and verification order.

The package records the future apps/ui-xr-iwsdk-spike app as contract-only and non-runnable until operator install-scope approval, exact IWSDK versions, accepted transitive license posture, and the IWSDK policy lane are in place.

The package exposes a pre-install package policy and evaluator that accepts exact-versioned first-slice @iwsdk/core and @iwsdk/xr-input proposals while flagging unpinned versions, blocked packages, blocked license expressions, and blocked transitive packages before workspace package manifests or lockfiles change.

The pre-install evaluator rejects @iwsdk packages outside the unattended first-slice allowlist, treats review-required packages such as GLXF, UIKitML, Meta Spatial, locomotion, dev tooling, and GLTF optimization as not ready for unattended install, blocks sharp-libvips transitive variants, and normalizes blocked license matching case-insensitively.
The root workspace exposes pnpm iwsdk:preinstall as a JSON preflight report command that can score the default first-slice proposal or a proposal JSON file without installing IWSDK dependencies.

The root workspace runs the default pnpm iwsdk:preinstall report, pnpm iwsdk:workspace:posture scan, and latest-snapshot pnpm iwsdk:evidence:validate check inside pnpm iwsdk:verify, and architecture tests enforce that the opt-in IWSDK lane continues to include the preinstall/workspace/evidence gates while staying outside the default pnpm verify script and keeping blocked IWSDK packages out of manifests and the lockfile.
claims_not_supported: That IWSDK has been installed as a production or spike app dependency in the main workspace.
That the IWSDK MCP runtime has been configured for Codex in this workspace.
That IWSDK has passed physical Quest 3 frame-pacing, thermal, comfort, or controller-behavior validation.
That license/procurement blockers for @meta-quest/hzdb, sharp/libvips, or unknown pmndrs metadata are resolved.
